mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real personal consumption expenditures indicates structural demand weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint divergence shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2% quarter-over-quarter during a monetary tightening cycle signals resilient supply-side capacity that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate expanding construction activity and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5d returns with a win rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Real personal consumption expenditures growth above 1.5% during a post-shock recovery phase reflects robust consumer spending that directly lifts corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth above 1.5% fails to predict positive returns during recovery periods.
